plans.name: Plan A
plans.description: A conservative approach focusing on cost containment by instructing vessels to reduce speeds and loitering in secondary anchorage zones while waiting for port reopening.
details: Reduce vessel speed to 12 knots (slow-steaming) to minimize fuel burn and arrival at the 45-mile queue.
Divert Shanghai-bound vessels to Kaohsiung or Busan for 'bunker-and-wait' positioning to avoid East China Sea fog risks.
Implement immediate 14-day furlough for Line Alpha assembly staff to preserve operational cash flow during the OLED stockout.
Maintain current maritime bookings to avoid 'spot market' price surges, accepting the projected $96.6M revenue at risk.

plans.name: Plan B
plans.description: A balanced strategy utilizing the China-Europe Railway Express for EU routes and feeder-diversion through Busan for North American cargo.
details: Reroute Shanghai → Hamburg/Rotterdam cargo via DB Schenker rail links departing from Xi'an or Chengdu hubs.
Utilize cross-border trucking (CBX) to move Ningbo-origin cargo to Haiphong, Vietnam for secondary vessel loading.
Leverage KRPUS (Busan) as a primary transshipment node for Long Beach cargo, bypassing the CNSHG Donghai Bridge blockade.
Negotiate priority berthing with HMM or Maersk at secondary ports to mitigate the 5-7 day diversion delays.

plans.name: Plan C
plans.description: An aggressive, high-speed recovery plan to bypass maritime bottlenecks entirely using dedicated air cargo for critical OLED and Li-Ion components.
details: Secure immediate Boeing 747-8F charter capacity via Atlas Air or Kalitta Air for 12,000 OLED modules to meet the March 8th deadline.
Bypass PVG congestion by trucking high-value electronics to Zhengzhou (CGO) or Hong Kong (HKG) for immediate air export.
Establish a 'Green Channel' for customs clearance at LAX and FRA to ensure 4-hour tarmac-to-factory transit times.
Execute 'split-shipment' protocols: send 20% of critical inventory via air to sustain production while Plan B handles the remaining 80%.
